rulings.Case Title: ADA SORTO ET AL VS. WEDGEWOOD, INC. ET AL

rulings.Case Title: ANSELMO SAUCEDO ET AL VS. BISMARCK NUNEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Medical Records Of Defendant Flores; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Declaration Of Carlos Bocanegra; Separate Statement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 28, 2017, line 2. DEFENDANT BISMARCK NUNEZ, SANDY FLORES' Amended Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Medical Records Of Defendant Flores GRANTED. Requested information appears to be in violation of Federal Laws. See Bhogatia v. Altamonte (2014) 765 F.3d 1277 and Joint Statement. Plaintiff failed to articulate what information is being sought that would not be in violation of the authorities cited by Defendant. Pages 11-18 of Defendant's Amended Memorandum of Points and Authorities filed on March 13, 2017 are stricken for violation of CRC 3.1113 (d) and (f). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 28, 2017, line 3. PLAINTIFF LORNA GIDDA Plaintiff'S Motion To Vacate Dismissal With Prejudice GRANTED. No opposition filed. Dismissal entered on February 7, 2017 is set aside. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: U.S.BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AS TRUSTEE FOR VS. CARLOTA DEL PORTILLO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Permitting Litigation Of Permitting Litigation Of Title Upon Which Right Of Possession Is Based And Requiring Further Responses From Plaintiff To Related Discovery And Otherwise; And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, March 28, 2017, line 4. DEFENDANT CARLOTA DEL PORTILLO, ELENA ASTURIAS' Motion Permitting Litigation Of Permitting Litigation Of Title Upon Which Right Of Possession Is Based And Requiring Further Responses From Plaintiff To Related Discovery And Otherwise; And For Sanctions DENIED in part and hearing required in part. The motion permitting litigation of title is DENIED to the extent Defendants are seeking litigation of title beyond limits set forth in Cheney v. Trauzettel (1937) 9 Cal.2d 158. Hearing Required to address why Plaintiff should not be required to provide responses without objections in light of responses being late. Parties are ordered to appear at 8:30 a.m. and engage in meaningful meet and confer efforts to resolve this discovery motion in light of Court's ruling on the motion permitting litigation of title. Parties of report of their progress at 9:30 a.m. = (501/REQ)
